title: Iran says 'no decision' on whether to attend next round of US talks
description: Pakistan moved ahead Monday with preparations for a new round of talks between the United States and Iran days before a tenuous ceasefire is set to expire, even as renewed conflict around the Strait of Hormuz raised questions about whether the meeting would take place. FRANCE 24 Reza Sayah reports from Tehran, Iran.

title: US Navy seizure of Iranian ship casts doubt on fresh ceasefire talks in Pakistan
description: The US Navy's forcible seizure of an Iranian-flagged cargo ships threw doubt on an announcement from President Donald Trump that U.S. negotiators will head to Pakistan on Monday for another round of talks with Iran. Trump's announcement Sunday had raised hopes of extending a fragile ceasefire set to expire by Wednesday, but Iran has not confirmed it will attend. FRANCE 24's International Affairs Editor Andrew Hilliar tells us more.

title: Food security under threat as Middle East war disrupts fertiliser supply
description: Around one-third of global fertiliser production comes from the Gulf region, making the area’s stability crucial for global food supply chains. With the Strait of Hormuz, an essential transit route for energy and commodities, facing repeated disruptions, concerns are growing over the impact on fertiliser exports and deliveries worldwide. FRANCE 24's Shirli Sitbon has the analysis.

title: Iran war: What is happening on day 51 of the US-Israeli conflict?
description: Tehran says it will keep the strategic Strait of Hormuz closed until Washington ends its blockade of Iranian ports.

title: Trump says US negotiators to head to Pakistan for Iran ceasefire talks
description: Trump announces talks in Islamabad despite accusing Iran of violating truce, as he warns Tehran of severe repercussions.

title: Iran War Live Updates: Trump Says U.S. Officials Will Travel to Pakistan for Talks
description: President Trump said the officials would arrive Monday evening in Pakistan, which has been mediating negotiations. The Strait of Hormuz remained largely closed on Sunday, after Iran said the parties were far from a final deal to end the war.

title: New site offers travel by armchair to Eilat’s magnificent coral reefs
description:
Website aimed at raising awareness about Gulf of Eilat's marine wonders; includes virtual safaris through different underwater habitats, a reef live cam, scientific research

title: Humans far behind as robot breaks record at Beijing half marathon
description: A humanoid robot competing against flesh-and-blood runners broke the world record at a Beijing half marathon on Sunday, showcasing the rapid technological advancement achieved by Chinese makers. Humanoid robots have become a common sight in China in recent years, in the media as well as in public spaces.

title: Pope Leo tells Angola during huge Mass to ‘build hope’
description: On the third leg of his Africa tour, Leo urges Angola to move beyond divisions created by its war-scarred past.

title: Iran war: 10 frequently used words and their meanings
description: The US-Israel war on Iran nears two months.  We break down some of the frequently used terminology in this war.

title: Does the world need new leaders? Varsha Gandikota & Naledi Pandor
description: Varsha Gandikota-Nellutla asks former South African Foreign Minister Naledi Pandor if global leadership is in crisis.
